That said, we wore them as bridesmaids dresses for a Vegas wedding and they look fantastic in pictures, have just enough stretch, and the fabric drapes nicely. I am 5’6” 155lbs 39” bust 28” waist 40” hips and the small fit perfectly. The dress landed around my knees and the slit came pretty high but it didn’t look bad.

STUNNING! This dress just DOES all of the things! It hits in all the right places and accentuates perfectly! This dress leaves you subject to your figure (or your spanx). It's not flattering at all. I feel it didn't flow widely from the hips like it looks on the image. I thought it would open into a flowing skirt from the hips (A-line style), but it sticks to your body all the way down, until maybe the knee. And I dont think it was a wrong size because I sized up and everywhere else it felt loose enough or even too loose. The fabric is synthetic, very stretchy and thin, which I think is what doesn't help smooth your figure since it has 0 compression or thickness to hide imperfections, but it is of course comfortable if that's what you seek. The knot in the waist is a redeeming quality since it does camouflage a belly. I didn't have very high expectations, I bought for a barbie-themed party. The shiny fabric was nice, the contrast of the gold and purple sort of iridescent shimmer was also very pretty and would have been perfect for my party. But it just looked terrible on my body, even with shapewear. The length was midi. I am 5'4" and it hit between mid-calf and ankle. [I was not going to upload pics because I really dont like how I look, plus my niece drew all over the mirror, but I guess you had to judge for yourself whether it's what you want]. Ugh, chest opening! This dress looked cute and while I am bigger than the model (size 12 bottom/14 top, 38DD, 5'3 height), I was hoping it would look well. PROS: Dress has a long back zipper, stitching is well done, the material is great, I think it fits well and I was told it looked sexy. CON: Off of the left shoulder, there is a slight shoulder opening which is part of the design, however, as a result of this, the "crossover wrap" around the chest is not closed. This creates a weird "opening/bulge" on my left breast (see photos). This may not be a problem if you have smaller breasts, but at 38DD, this is a problem. I'd say a bigger size may work but if you look at the pictures, the dress is already slightly lose on the back and hips. Overall, great design but not for a large chest with smaller waist.

Gorgeous floor-length dress, fits well depending on body type This dress looked so beautiful online, and I still do love it in person. The color was indeed wine-y but with a slight purple to it. The bottom zips up right above the hips and the rest truly is tightened by the lace up bodice on the back, so make sure you have someone to lace you up. I ordered a 14, which is usually what I am wearing these days. It fit a little tight at the zipper but a little looser than desired on the top. The material is not stretchy and is very high quality evening gown material, so the dress cannot actually be tightened at the bodice or it will bunch up and not fall right. So if you are busty, I would say you are good for this fit. But if your weight is more in your waist or belly, the proportions may not flatter your particular body type. The dress shape, material and color were gorgeous though, and it is floor length even with heels (I'm 5'7"), so is a real showstopper.

Bump friendly lightweight dress This dress made room for my 21 week bump perfectly. Other than that it is just ok. I would say the model images are not realistic as the fabric is thinner than it looks, drapes loosely and does not lay or gather anything like the seller shows. There are also seaming issues that lessen the quality. I can see this being a nice option for professional photos since the camera likely wouldn’t pick up those details, but not quite suitable for a formal event. True to size though and workable with heels at 5’2”. The pink color is awful, green is better. I would need to get creative with the neckline (did not clip the manufacturer tack since I wasn’t keeping it).

This dress makes me feel Anita in a community theatre production of West Side Story: there's something costumey about it, which might be suitable for prom or other formal occasion. It's surprisingly comfortable and easy to wear, and the slit is modest. The dress is stretch and runs large. You do need to have an ample bosom or padded pushup bra to fill the bodice. For reference, I'm 5'8", 120 lbs, 34A+ bust, 26 waist, 35 hip, 33" inseam and ordered a small. I need to have it taken for a proper fit, but could probably wear it as-is if I pin the décolletage closed and wear the right undergarments. Perhaps because it's such an attention-getter I'll have it altered. The fabric is stretchy and I wouldn't attempt it myself with my limited sewing skills. It's festive and forgiving. High fashion types might look down upon it, so know your audience.

Stunning! I felt like a Queen in this dress! This dress makes me feel like a Queen! Even my uncle told me he thought I looked like a Queen! It’s so beautiful, elegant, sexy, and sophisticated. Simple yet a showstopper! I wore it to my mom’s 85th birthday party and got tons of compliments! You have to be in great shape to wear it. Sorry but it’s the truth! I’m 53 but I have exercised, taken care of myself and eaten healthy for over 20 years. Although the material is not too thin, it still has spandex in it, and it’s VERY fitting. So you need to either be in great shape or be SUPREMELY confident because it WILL show every single curve and/or other thing you may or may not feel comfortable with. I happen to love it on me. I didn’t wear a bra because the back is a little too low for a strapless and there isn’t enough support in the bodice to hold a pair of adhesive “chicken cutlets” (LOL), as I call them! But it’s a black dress so I wasn’t worried about the “girls” being loose or the nips making an appearance! For reference, I’m 5’7”, 126 lbs., 34C-27-37, and I ordered a Small. It fit me perfectly because I wear a small size in pretty much everything. I say buy it! It’s very glamorous. Considering I paid so little for it, I felt like it was a red carpet-worthy dress!

Comfortable and Flattering This dress seems to be made very well. I found no areas with odd bunching, loose threads, or uneven seams. The material is also a nice thickness with enough stretch to be quite forgiving, and the zipper works flawlessly every time, even when I’m zipping it up and down by myself. I got this dress in green, and it’s a lovely, dark shade that’s elegant rather than bland. The design is slimming and sexy (those were the first things my husband said when I tried it on), feels extremely comfortable to wear, and isn’t constrictive when I move. The neckline is flattering, and the slit is intriguing since it isn’t the typical side style. I’m short, so I had some concerns about how that would land on me. It isn’t too high, but it shows more of my aging thighs than I like, so I’ll need to wear pantyhose. That isn’t typically something I like, but it’s an easy concession to make for this dress. Regarding fit, I’m 5’3” and typically wear anywhere from a U.S. size 4 to 8, depending on the brand and cut of the clothing. I followed the size chart to order a medium, and it fits perfectly. I seriously love everything about this dress. I can’t recommend it enough.

This dress snatches me in all the right places! I'm 5'2", 150 lbs, and typically wear size 6-8 in jeans. Lovely surprise This dress surprised me in a few ways. I have super low expectations when it comes to Amazon clothing. This isn’t the best quality, it feels like cheap polyester that so many Amazon clothes do. But it doesn’t LOOK cheap. The design and pattern make the dress appear much nicer than it really is. It’s also extremely flattering. In this photo, I’m 180lbs (5’5”) and I have a significant mom pouch from 2 c-sections. I immediately felt very confident in my appearance upon trying this on. The front of the dress has a snap closure which makes it nursing friendly. Given the extreme lightweight polyester fabric, this skirt will fly up at the slightest breeze, so just something to keep in mind given how short the length is. I wore something similar (also from Amazon) to an outdoor wedding last fall and I spent the majority of my time trying to hold down my skirt. Overall, I highly recommended this for a quick and inexpensive outfit!

Beyond beautiful & long enough for tall girls! This dress was everything I needed for my glamorous Christmas photos with my babies. I purchased 5 dresses because it’s really hard for me to purchase dresses lurch less online. I’m not a dress girl. Let’s make that real clear. I am a T-shirt and leggings/jeans type of girl, so I was really worried that none of them would be flattering or comfortable enough. I won’t lie, I loved every single dress that I ordered off Amazon. The quality was great, but we’re here to talk about this one. The one that I chose at the end of the day. It’s very stretchy and comfortable. The slit up the side is just sexy enough to keep you from showing off too much But still make you look like one hot mama. I wore a body shaper that’s basically like underwear that goes all the way up to the bottom of your bra because I didn’t want it to show through this fabric. It is thick and made really well but I knew that if I got anything like a corset style or anything super thick it would show through the dress for sure. some of you may not need that extra Shapeware but after two kids this mom needed just a little smoothing as it kind of shows all curves. Anyway, as far as length, it was almost too long, even with 6 inch heels and I am around 5’7”-5’8”ish without the heels. That’s one thing I loved about it because long dresses are usually too short on me. I also got these beautiful sparkly rhinestone heels off Amazon and they went beautifully with this dress. At the end of the day I highly recommend this dress as it’s really great quality, has a great stretch, really comfortable and so damn sexy!

Exactly what I was looking for! This dress was everything that I was looking for! Do I love it? Yes! Would I recommend it? Absolutely! Would I buy another one? Probably not. For reference, I am 5'5, about 165-170lbs, and a 36D in cup size. Much of my weight is on my behind, love handles, and stomach area. This dress material is thick. I never had an issue with the zipper, as others had stated. However, I did forewarn my husband to please be careful because of the reviews. I was wearing a skim underneath, which helped tremendously! The dress did not have a foul smell; everything was sewn perfectly, and the length was exactly what I wanted. The cons- I do not consider this dress to be of "bandage" type material. It is much stretchier than the actual bandage type. The halter piece is exceptionally long, and because of the fabric, it is stretchy. I would like to see something that supports my girls more. An XL might have been a better option for my torso and down, but not my bust area. So, I think the L was an "okay" choice, too. Lastly, I was wearing a bra, and in other pictures, you can see the lines through the dress, which I was not happy with. The pros- I love the assortment of colors the seller offers because finding a maroon-colored dress that matched my college color was challenging. Seeing that it was stretchy, it was easy to move in, but I disliked the bust area so much. It was bigger in that area. Overall, it is a beautiful dress, and I love my pictures. However, if the dress was more "bandage" than stretchy, better made for the bust area, and the halter piece was stronger, I may have given the dress a 5 star rating.

Very pretty dress. Perfect. This dress was exactly as I hoped. The quality of the fabric and stitching is good. In the 3rd picture you can see the lining in the bodice. It is very comfortable, snug around the lower ribs, waistline is just above my natural waistline, as most dresses of this style are. This means it doesn't squeeze at the waist, which is nice. I got a size small. I am 5'4, 123-ish pounds. I was concerned about the bodice with this style. I am a 34A and this style bodice usually doesn't fit real well no matter what. This fits but not as snug as I would like, but that is what I expected. It comes a bit low on me for my comfort so I put an insert in the neckline (first picture). I think it still looks nice, though. In the first picture I have a petticoat on underneath, in the second picture I do not. I think it looks good both ways, even without it. The blue color with the white dots looks great. Love the color. When I first put it on the seam at the bottom of the bodice was folded up and made the fabric look bunched up. When I figured out why it looked that way, I just folded the seam down inside and it is fine. I have not yet steamed this dress so the wrinkles you see are how it came out of the package, it has been hanging for a few days but that is it. Needs to be steamed but doesn't look too bad. The length is perfect, exactly what I wanted. I would definitely recommend this dress.
